article_title: Immigration Officer or other official:

resolved_references.content:
Article 16
(1) Immigration officials refuse a person to leave Indonesian territory if the person:
a. does not have valid and valid Travel Documents;
b. necessary for the purposes of investigations and inquiries at the request of authorized officials; or
c. his name is on the Prevention list.
(2) Immigration officials also have the authority to refuse foreigners to leave Indonesian territory if the foreigner still has obligations in Indonesia which must be completed in accordance with the provisions of the laws and regulations.

Part Four
Obligations of the Person in Charge of the Transport Equipment

Article 67
(1) Immigration control of Indonesian citizens is carried out at the time of request for Travel Documents, leaving or entering, or being outside the Indonesian Territory, by:
a. collection, processing and presentation of data and information;
b. preparing a list of names of Indonesian citizens who are subject to prevention from leaving Indonesian territory;
c. monitoring every Indonesian citizen who applies for Travel Documents, leaving or entering Indonesian Territory, and who is outside Indonesian Territory; and
d. taking photos and fingerprints.
(2) The results of Immigration supervision as intended in paragraph (1) constitute Immigration data which can be determined as confidential data.

Article 70
(1) Immigration Officials or those appointed in the context of Immigration supervision as intended in Article 67 and Article 68 are obliged to carry out:
a. collecting data on Immigration services, both Indonesian citizens and foreign citizens;
b. collecting traffic data, both Indonesian citizens and foreign nationals entering or leaving Indonesian Territory;
c. collecting data on foreign nationals who have received detention decisions, both in the Immigration Detention Room at the Immigration Office and at the Immigration Detention Center; and
d. collecting data on foreign citizens who are in the process of immigration enforcement.
(2) Data collection as intended in paragraph (1) is carried out by entering data into the Immigration Management Information System which was built and developed by the Directorate General.

Article 118
Any Guarantor who deliberately provides false information or does not fulfill the guarantee provided as intended in Article 63 paragraph (2) and paragraph (3) shall be punished with a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of IDR 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah).

Article 119
(1) Every Foreigner who enters and/or remains in Indonesian Territory who does not have valid and valid Travel Documents and Visa as intended in Article 8 shall be punished with a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of IDR 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah).
(2) Every Foreigner who intentionally uses a Travel Document, but knows or reasonably suspects that the Travel Document is fake or falsified, shall be punished with a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of Rp. 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah).

Article 120
(1) Any person who commits an act aimed at seeking profit, either directly or indirectly, for himself or for another person by bringing a person or group of people, whether organized or unorganized, or ordering another person to bring a person or group of people, whether organized or unorganized, who does not have the legal right to enter the Indonesian Territory or leave the Indonesian Territory and/or enter the territory of another country, where the person does not have the right to enter the territory legally, whether using valid documents or fake documents, or without using Travel Documents, either through immigration inspection or not, be punished for Human Smuggling with a minimum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum of 15 (fifteen) years and a fine of at least IDR 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah) and a maximum of IDR 1,500,000,000.00 (one billion five hundred million rupiah).
(2) Attempts to commit the crime of Human Smuggling are punished with the same crime as intended in paragraph (1).

Article 121
Sentenced to a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of Rp. 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah):
a. any person who deliberately makes a fake or falsifies a Visa or Entry Certificate or Stay Permit with the intention of using it for himself or another person to enter or leave or remain in the Territory of Indonesia;
b. any Foreigner who deliberately uses a fake or falsified Visa or Entry Certificate or Residence Permit to enter or leave or remain in the Territory of Indonesia.

Article 122
Sentenced to a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of Rp. 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah):
a. any Foreigner who deliberately misuses or carries out activities that are not in accordance with the aims and objectives of the Stay Permit granted to him;
b. any person who orders or provides an opportunity for a Foreigner to abuse or carry out activities that are not in accordance with the aim or purpose of granting the Stay Permit given to him.

Article 123
Sentenced to a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of Rp. 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah):
a. any person who deliberately provides false or falsified documents or data or incorrect information with the intention of obtaining a Visa or Residence Permit for himself or another person;
b. every Foreigner who deliberately uses a Visa or Stay Permit as intended in letter a to enter and/or reside in Indonesian Territory.

Article 127
Any person who intentionally and unlawfully keeps a fake or falsified Republic of Indonesia Travel Document with the intention of using it for himself or another person shall be punished with a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of IDR 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah).

Article 128
Sentenced to a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of Rp. 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah):
a. any person who intentionally and unlawfully prints, possesses, stores or trades blank Republic of Indonesia Travel Documents or other Immigration Documents;
b. any person who intentionally and unlawfully makes, owns, keeps, or trades stamps or other devices used to validate Republic of Indonesia Travel Documents or other Immigration Documents.

Article 129
Any person who intentionally and unlawfully for the benefit of himself or another person destroys, changes, adds to, reduces or removes, either in whole or in part, information or stamps contained in a Republic of Indonesia Travel Document or other Immigration Document, shall be punished by a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of IDR 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah).

Article 131
Any person who intentionally without rights and against the law possesses, stores, destroys, deletes, changes, duplicates, uses and/or accesses Immigration data, either manually or electronically, for the benefit of himself or another person shall be punished by a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of IDR 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah).

Article 132
An Immigration Officer or other appointed official who intentionally and unlawfully provides Republic of Indonesia Travel Documents and/or provides or extends Immigration Documents to someone he knows is not entitled shall be punished by imprisonment for a maximum of 7 (seven) years.

Article 134
Any Detainee who intentionally:
a. making, possessing, using, and/or distributing weapons is punishable by a maximum imprisonment of 3 (three) years;
b. Running away from an Immigration Detention Center or Immigration Detention Room is punishable by a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years.

Article 135
Every person who enters into a pseudo-marriage with the aim of obtaining Immigration Documents and/or obtaining citizenship status of the Republic of Indonesia shall be punished with a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of IDR 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah).

final_chunk:
[ORIGINAL Article 133]
Article 133
Immigration Officer or other official:
a. allowing someone to commit an Immigration crime as intended in Article 118, Article 119, Article 120, Article 121, Article 122, Article 123, Article 126, Article 127, Article 128, Article 129, Article 131, Article 132, Article 133 letter b, Article 134 letter b, and Article 135 which he should know shall be punished with a maximum prison sentence. 5 (five) years;
b. intentionally leaking confidential Immigration data to unauthorized parties as intended in Article 67 paragraph (2) and Article 68 paragraph (2) shall be punished with a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years;
c. intentionally not carrying out standard operating procedures that apply in the departure or arrival inspection process at the Immigration Checkpoint which results in the entry of Foreigners into the Indonesian Territory as intended in Article 13 paragraph (1) or the exit of the person from the Indonesian Territory as intended in Article 16 paragraph (1) shall be punished with imprisonment for a maximum of 2 (two) years;
d. intentionally and unlawfully failing to carry out standard operating procedures for guarding a Detainee in an Immigration Detention Center or Immigration Detention Room which results in the Detainee escaping shall be punished by imprisonment for a maximum of 2 (two) years;
e. intentionally and unlawfully not entering data into the Immigration Management Information System as intended in Article 70 shall be punished with imprisonment for a maximum of 6 (six) months.

[DEFINITION FROM Article 13]
Article 13
(1) Immigration Officials refuse foreigners to enter Indonesian Territory if the foreigner:
a. his name is listed on the Deterrence list;
b. does not have valid and valid Travel Documents;
c. having fake Immigration documents;
d. do not have a Visa, except those who are exempt from the obligation to have a Visa;
e. has provided false information in obtaining a visa;
f. suffering from an infectious disease that endangers public health;
g. involved in international crimes and organized transnational criminal acts;
h. included in the wanted list of persons to be arrested from a foreign country;
i. involved in treasonous activities against the Government of the Republic of Indonesia; or
j. including in a network of practices or activities of prostitution, human trafficking and human smuggling.
(2) Foreigners who are refused entry as intended in paragraph (1) are placed under temporary supervision awaiting the repatriation process concerned.

[DEFINITION FROM Article 16]
Article 16
(1) Immigration officials refuse a person to leave Indonesian territory if the person:
a. does not have valid and valid Travel Documents;
b. necessary for the purposes of investigations and inquiries at the request of authorized officials; or
c. his name is on the Prevention list.
(2) Immigration officials also have the authority to refuse foreigners to leave Indonesian territory if the foreigner still has obligations in Indonesia which must be completed in accordance with the provisions of the laws and regulations.

Part Four

(1) Immigration control of Indonesian citizens is carried out at the time of request for Travel Documents, leaving or entering, or being outside the Indonesian Territory, by:

d. taking photos and fingerprints.
(2) The results of Immigration supervision as intended in paragraph (1) constitute Immigration data which can be determined as confidential data.

[DEFINITION FROM Article 68]
Article 68
(1) Immigration control of Foreigners is carried out at the time of Visa application, entry or exit, and the granting of a Stay Permit is carried out by:
a. collection, processing and presentation of data and information;
b. preparation of a list of names of Foreigners subject to Deterrence or Prevention;
c. supervision of the presence and activities of Foreigners in Indonesian Territory;
d. taking photos and fingerprints; and
e. other activities that can be legally accounted for.
(2) The results of Immigration supervision as intended in paragraph (1) constitute Immigration data which can be determined as confidential data.

[DEFINITION FROM Article 70]
Article 70
(1) Immigration Officials or those appointed in the context of Immigration supervision as intended in Article 67 and Article 68 are obliged to carry out:
a. collecting data on Immigration services, both Indonesian citizens and foreign citizens;
b. collecting traffic data, both Indonesian citizens and foreign nationals entering or leaving Indonesian Territory;
c. collecting data on foreign nationals who have received detention decisions, both in the Immigration Detention Room at the Immigration Office and at the Immigration Detention Center; and
d. collecting data on foreign citizens who are in the process of immigration enforcement.
(2) Data collection as intended in paragraph (1) is carried out by entering data into the Immigration Management Information System which was built and developed by the Directorate General.

Article 118
Any Guarantor who deliberately provides false information or does not fulfill the guarantee provided as intended in Article 63 paragraph (2) and paragraph (3) shall be punished with a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of IDR 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah).

(1) Every Foreigner who enters and/or remains in Indonesian Territory who does not have valid and valid Travel Documents and Visa as intended in Article 8 shall be punished with a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of IDR 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah).
(2) Every Foreigner who intentionally uses a Travel Document, but knows or reasonably suspects that the Travel Document is fake or falsified, shall be punished with a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of Rp. 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah).

[DEFINITION FROM Article 120]
Article 120
(1) Any person who commits an act aimed at seeking profit, either directly or indirectly, for himself or for another person by bringing a person or group of people, whether organized or unorganized, or ordering another person to bring a person or group of people, whether organized or unorganized, who does not have the legal right to enter the Indonesian Territory or leave the Indonesian Territory and/or enter the territory of another country, where the person does not have the right to enter the territory legally, whether using valid documents or fake documents, or without using Travel Documents, either through immigration inspection or not, be punished for Human Smuggling with a minimum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum of 15 (fifteen) years and a fine of at least IDR 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah) and a maximum of IDR 1,500,000,000.00 (one billion five hundred million rupiah).
(2) Attempts to commit the crime of Human Smuggling are punished with the same crime as intended in paragraph (1).

[DEFINITION FROM Article 121]
Article 121
Sentenced to a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of Rp. 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah):
a. any person who deliberately makes a fake or falsifies a Visa or Entry Certificate or Stay Permit with the intention of using it for himself or another person to enter or leave or remain in the Territory of Indonesia;
b. any Foreigner who deliberately uses a fake or falsified Visa or Entry Certificate or Residence Permit to enter or leave or remain in the Territory of Indonesia.

[DEFINITION FROM Article 122]
Article 122
Sentenced to a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of Rp. 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah):
a. any Foreigner who deliberately misuses or carries out activities that are not in accordance with the aims and objectives of the Stay Permit granted to him;
b. any person who orders or provides an opportunity for a Foreigner to abuse or carry out activities that are not in accordance with the aim or purpose of granting the Stay Permit given to him.

[DEFINITION FROM Article 123]
Article 123
Sentenced to a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of Rp. 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah):
a. any person who deliberately provides false or falsified documents or data or incorrect information with the intention of obtaining a Visa or Residence Permit for himself or another person;
b. every Foreigner who deliberately uses a Visa or Stay Permit as intended in letter a to enter and/or reside in Indonesian Territory.

[DEFINITION FROM Article 126]
Article 126
Any person who intentionally:
a. using Republic of Indonesia Travel Documents to enter or leave Indonesian Territory, but knowing or reasonably suspecting that the Republic of Indonesia Travel Documents are fake or falsified, shall be punished with a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of IDR 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah);
b. using another person's Republic of Indonesia Travel Document or one that has been revoked or declared invalid to enter or leave the Territory of Indonesia or handing over to another person a Republic of Indonesia Travel Document that was given to him or another person with the intention of using it unlawfully shall be punished by a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of Rp. 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah);
c. providing invalid data or incorrect information to obtain a Republic of Indonesia Travel Document for himself or another person shall be punished with a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of Rp. 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah);
d. possessing or unlawfully using 2 (two) or more similar Republic of Indonesia Travel Documents and all of which are still valid shall be punished with a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of IDR 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah);
e. falsifying Republic of Indonesia Travel Documents or creating fake Republic of Indonesia Travel Documents with the intention of using them for oneself or another person shall be punished with a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of Rp. 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah).

[DEFINITION FROM Article 127]
Article 127
Any person who intentionally and unlawfully keeps a fake or falsified Republic of Indonesia Travel Document with the intention of using it for himself or another person shall be punished with a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of IDR 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah).

[DEFINITION FROM Article 128]
Article 128
Sentenced to a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of Rp. 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah):
a. any person who intentionally and unlawfully prints, possesses, stores or trades blank Republic of Indonesia Travel Documents or other Immigration Documents;
b. any person who intentionally and unlawfully makes, owns, keeps, or trades stamps or other devices used to validate Republic of Indonesia Travel Documents or other Immigration Documents.

[DEFINITION FROM Article 129]
Article 129
Any person who intentionally and unlawfully for the benefit of himself or another person destroys, changes, adds to, reduces or removes, either in whole or in part, information or stamps contained in a Republic of Indonesia Travel Document or other Immigration Document, shall be punished by a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of IDR 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah).

[DEFINITION FROM Article 131]
Article 131
Any person who intentionally without rights and against the law possesses, stores, destroys, deletes, changes, duplicates, uses and/or accesses Immigration data, either manually or electronically, for the benefit of himself or another person shall be punished by a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of IDR 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah).

[DEFINITION FROM Article 132]
Article 132
An Immigration Officer or other appointed official who intentionally and unlawfully provides Republic of Indonesia Travel Documents and/or provides or extends Immigration Documents to someone he knows is not entitled shall be punished by imprisonment for a maximum of 7 (seven) years.

[DEFINITION FROM Article 134]
Article 134
Any Detainee who intentionally:
a. making, possessing, using, and/or distributing weapons is punishable by a maximum imprisonment of 3 (three) years;
b. Running away from an Immigration Detention Center or Immigration Detention Room is punishable by a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years.

[DEFINITION FROM Article 135]
Article 135
Every person who enters into a pseudo-marriage with the aim of obtaining Immigration Documents and/or obtaining citizenship status of the Republic of Indonesia shall be punished with a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of IDR 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah).

[DEFINITION FROM Article 16]
Article 16
(1) Immigration officials refuse a person to leave Indonesian territory if the person:
a. does not have valid and valid Travel Documents;
b. necessary for the purposes of investigations and inquiries at the request of authorized officials; or
c. his name is on the Prevention list.
(2) Immigration officials also have the authority to refuse foreigners to leave Indonesian territory if the foreigner still has obligations in Indonesia which must be completed in accordance with the provisions of the laws and regulations.

Part Four
Obligations of the Person in Charge of the Transport Equipment

[DEFINITION FROM Article 67]
Article 67
(1) Immigration control of Indonesian citizens is carried out at the time of request for Travel Documents, leaving or entering, or being outside the Indonesian Territory, by:
a. collection, processing and presentation of data and information;
b. preparing a list of names of Indonesian citizens who are subject to prevention from leaving Indonesian territory;
c. monitoring every Indonesian citizen who applies for Travel Documents, leaving or entering Indonesian Territory, and who is outside Indonesian Territory; and
d. taking photos and fingerprints.
(2) The results of Immigration supervision as intended in paragraph (1) constitute Immigration data which can be determined as confidential data.

[DEFINITION FROM Article 70]
Article 70
(1) Immigration Officials or those appointed in the context of Immigration supervision as intended in Article 67 and Article 68 are obliged to carry out:
a. collecting data on Immigration services, both Indonesian citizens and foreign citizens;
b. collecting traffic data, both Indonesian citizens and foreign nationals entering or leaving Indonesian Territory;
c. collecting data on foreign nationals who have received detention decisions, both in the Immigration Detention Room at the Immigration Office and at the Immigration Detention Center; and
d. collecting data on foreign citizens who are in the process of immigration enforcement.
(2) Data collection as intended in paragraph (1) is carried out by entering data into the Immigration Management Information System which was built and developed by the Directorate General.

[DEFINITION FROM Article 118]
Article 118
Any Guarantor who deliberately provides false information or does not fulfill the guarantee provided as intended in Article 63 paragraph (2) and paragraph (3) shall be punished with a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of IDR 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah).

Article 119

(2) Every Foreigner who intentionally uses a Travel Document, but knows or reasonably suspects that the Travel Document is fake or falsified, shall be punished with a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of Rp. 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah).

[ORIGINAL Article 133]
Article 133
Immigration Officer or other official:
a. allowing someone to commit an Immigration crime as intended in Article 118, Article 119, Article 120, Article 121, Article 122, Article 123, Article 126, Article 127, Article 128, Article 129, Article 131, Article 132, Article 133 letter b, Article 134 letter b, and Article 135 which he should know shall be punished with a maximum prison sentence. 5 (five) years;
b. intentionally leaking confidential Immigration data to unauthorized parties as intended in Article 67 paragraph (2) and Article 68 paragraph (2) shall be punished with a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years;
c. intentionally not carrying out standard operating procedures that apply in the departure or arrival inspection process at the Immigration Checkpoint which results in the entry of Foreigners into the Indonesian Territory as intended in Article 13 paragraph (1) or the exit of the person from the Indonesian Territory as intended in Article 16 paragraph (1) shall be punished with imprisonment for a maximum of 2 (two) years;
d. intentionally and unlawfully failing to carry out standard operating procedures for guarding a Detainee in an Immigration Detention Center or Immigration Detention Room which results in the Detainee escaping shall be punished by imprisonment for a maximum of 2 (two) years;
e. intentionally and unlawfully not entering data into the Immigration Management Information System as intended in Article 70 shall be punished with imprisonment for a maximum of 6 (six) months.

[DEFINITION FROM Article 13]
Article 13
(1) Immigration Officials refuse foreigners to enter Indonesian Territory if the foreigner:
a. his name is listed on the Deterrence list;
b. does not have valid and valid Travel Documents;
c. having fake Immigration documents;
d. do not have a Visa, except those who are exempt from the obligation to have a Visa;
e. has provided false information in obtaining a visa;
f. suffering from an infectious disease that endangers public health;
g. involved in international crimes and organized transnational criminal acts;
h. included in the wanted list of persons to be arrested from a foreign country;
i. involved in treasonous activities against the Government of the Republic of Indonesia; or
j. including in a network of practices or activities of prostitution, human trafficking and human smuggling.
(2) Foreigners who are refused entry as intended in paragraph (1) are placed under temporary supervision awaiting the repatriation process concerned.

[DEFINITION FROM Article 16]

article_title: Article 136
full_content:
Article 136
(1) In the event that criminal acts as intended in Article 114, Article 116, Article 117, Article 118, Article 120, Article 124, Article 128 and Article 129 are committed by a Corporation, the criminal shall be imposed on the management and the corporation.
(2) The only penalty imposed on a Corporation is a fine, provided that the amount of the fine is 3 (three) times the amount of each fine as intended in paragraph (1).
(3) The criminal provisions as intended in Article 113, Article 119, Article 121 letter b, Article 123 letter b, and Article 126 letters a and b do not apply to victims of human trafficking and human smuggling.

resolved_references.content:
Article 113
Every person who intentionally enters or leaves Indonesian Territory without being examined by an Immigration Officer at the Immigration Checkpoint as intended in Article 9 paragraph (1) shall be punished with a maximum imprisonment of 1 (one) year and/or a maximum fine of IDR 100,000,000.00 (one hundred million rupiah).

Article 114
(1) The Person in Charge of Transport Equipment entering or leaving the Indonesian Territory with means of transport that does not go through the Immigration Checkpoint as intended in Article 17 paragraph (1) shall be punished with a maximum imprisonment of 1 (one) year and/or a maximum fine of Rp. 100,000,000.00 (one hundred million rupiah).
(2) The Person in Charge of Transport Equipment who deliberately drops off or picks up passengers who do not go through inspection by the Immigration Officer or landing inspection officer at the Immigration Checkpoint as intended in Article 17 paragraph (2) shall be punished with a maximum imprisonment of 2 (two) years and/or a maximum fine of IDR 200,000,000.00 (two hundred million rupiah).

Article 116
Every Foreigner who does not carry out his obligations as intended in Article 71 shall be punished with imprisonment for a maximum of 3 (three) months or a fine of a maximum of Rp. 25,000,000.00 (twenty-five million rupiah).

Article 117
Owners or administrators of accommodation who do not provide information or do not provide data on Foreigners staying at home or at their accommodation after being asked by the Immigration Officer on duty as intended in Article 72 paragraph (2) shall be punished with imprisonment for a maximum of 3 (three) months or a fine of a maximum of Rp. 25,000,000.00 (twenty-five million rupiah).

Article 118

(2) Attempts to commit the crime of Human Smuggling are punished with the same crime as intended in paragraph (1).

Article 121
Sentenced to a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of Rp. 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah):
a. any person who deliberately makes a fake or falsifies a Visa or Entry Certificate or Stay Permit with the intention of using it for himself or another person to enter or leave or remain in the Territory of Indonesia;
b. any Foreigner who deliberately uses a fake or falsified Visa or Entry Certificate or Residence Permit to enter or leave or remain in the Territory of Indonesia.

Article 123
Sentenced to a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of Rp. 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah):
a. any person who deliberately provides false or falsified documents or data or incorrect information with the intention of obtaining a Visa or Residence Permit for himself or another person;
b. every Foreigner who deliberately uses a Visa or Stay Permit as intended in letter a to enter and/or reside in Indonesian Territory.

Article 124
Any person who deliberately hides or protects or provides lodging or provides a living or provides work to a foreigner who is known or reasonably suspected:
a. being in the Indonesian Territory illegally shall be punished with imprisonment for a maximum of 2 (two) years and/or a fine of a maximum of IDR 200,000,000.00 (two hundred million rupiah);
b. If your residence permit expires, you will be punished with a maximum imprisonment of 3 (three) months or a maximum fine of Rp. 25,000,000.00 (twenty-five million rupiah).

Article 128
Sentenced to a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of Rp. 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah):
a. any person who intentionally and unlawfully prints, possesses, stores or trades blank Republic of Indonesia Travel Documents or other Immigration Documents;
b. any person who intentionally and unlawfully makes, owns, keeps, or trades stamps or other devices used to validate Republic of Indonesia Travel Documents or other Immigration Documents.

Article 129
Any person who intentionally and unlawfully for the benefit of himself or another person destroys, changes, adds to, reduces or removes, either in whole or in part, information or stamps contained in a Republic of Indonesia Travel Document or other Immigration Document, shall be punished by a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of IDR 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah).

[ORIGINAL Article 136]
Article 136
(1) In the event that criminal acts as intended in Article 114, Article 116, Article 117, Article 118, Article 120, Article 124, Article 128 and Article 129 are committed by a Corporation, the criminal shall be imposed on the management and the corporation.
(2) The only penalty imposed on a Corporation is a fine, provided that the amount of the fine is 3 (three) times the amount of each fine as intended in paragraph (1).
(3) The criminal provisions as intended in Article 113, Article 119, Article 121 letter b, Article 123 letter b, and Article 126 letters a and b do not apply to victims of human trafficking and human smuggling.

[DEFINITION FROM Article 113]
Article 113
Every person who intentionally enters or leaves Indonesian Territory without being examined by an Immigration Officer at the Immigration Checkpoint as intended in Article 9 paragraph (1) shall be punished with a maximum imprisonment of 1 (one) year and/or a maximum fine of IDR 100,000,000.00 (one hundred million rupiah).

[DEFINITION FROM Article 114]
Article 114
(1) The Person in Charge of Transport Equipment entering or leaving the Indonesian Territory with means of transport that does not go through the Immigration Checkpoint as intended in Article 17 paragraph (1) shall be punished with a maximum imprisonment of 1 (one) year and/or a maximum fine of Rp. 100,000,000.00 (one hundred million rupiah).
(2) The Person in Charge of Transport Equipment who deliberately drops off or picks up passengers who do not go through inspection by the Immigration Officer or landing inspection officer at the Immigration Checkpoint as intended in Article 17 paragraph (2) shall be punished with a maximum imprisonment of 2 (two) years and/or a maximum fine of IDR 200,000,000.00 (two hundred million rupiah).

[DEFINITION FROM Article 116]
Article 116
Every Foreigner who does not carry out his obligations as intended in Article 71 shall be punished with imprisonment for a maximum of 3 (three) months or a fine of a maximum of Rp. 25,000,000.00 (twenty-five million rupiah).

[DEFINITION FROM Article 117]
Article 117
Owners or administrators of accommodation who do not provide information or do not provide data on Foreigners staying at home or at their accommodation after being asked by the Immigration Officer on duty as intended in Article 72 paragraph (2) shall be punished with imprisonment for a maximum of 3 (three) months or a fine of a maximum of Rp. 25,000,000.00 (twenty-five million rupiah).

[DEFINITION FROM Article 118]
Article 118
Any Guarantor who deliberately provides false information or does not fulfill the guarantee provided as intended in Article 63 paragraph (2) and paragraph (3) shall be punished with a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of IDR 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah).

[DEFINITION FROM Article 119]
Article 119
(1) Every Foreigner who enters and/or remains in Indonesian Territory who does not have valid and valid Travel Documents and Visa as intended in Article 8 shall be punished with a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of IDR 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah).
(2) Every Foreigner who intentionally uses a Travel Document, but knows or reasonably suspects that the Travel Document is fake or falsified, shall be punished with a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of Rp. 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah).

[DEFINITION FROM Article 120]
Article 120
(1) Any person who commits an act aimed at seeking profit, either directly or indirectly, for himself or for another person by bringing a person or group of people, whether organized or unorganized, or ordering another person to bring a person or group of people, whether organized or unorganized, who does not have the legal right to enter the Indonesian Territory or leave the Indonesian Territory and/or enter the territory of another country, where the person does not have the right to enter the territory legally, whether using valid documents or fake documents, or without using Travel Documents, either through immigration inspection or not, be punished for Human Smuggling with a minimum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum of 15 (fifteen) years and a fine of at least IDR 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah) and a maximum of IDR 1,500,000,000.00 (one billion five hundred million rupiah).
(2) Attempts to commit the crime of Human Smuggling are punished with the same crime as intended in paragraph (1).

Article 121

a. any person who deliberately makes a fake or falsifies a Visa or Entry Certificate or Stay Permit with the intention of using it for himself or another person to enter or leave or remain in the Territory of Indonesia;
b. any Foreigner who deliberately uses a fake or falsified Visa or Entry Certificate or Residence Permit to enter or leave or remain in the Territory of Indonesia.

a. any person who deliberately provides false or falsified documents or data or incorrect information with the intention of obtaining a Visa or Residence Permit for himself or another person;
b. every Foreigner who deliberately uses a Visa or Stay Permit as intended in letter a to enter and/or reside in Indonesian Territory.

Any person who deliberately hides or protects or provides lodging or provides a living or provides work to a foreigner who is known or reasonably suspected:
a. being in the Indonesian Territory illegally shall be punished with imprisonment for a maximum of 2 (two) years and/or a fine of a maximum of IDR 200,000,000.00 (two hundred million rupiah);
b. If your residence permit expires, you will be punished with a maximum imprisonment of 3 (three) months or a maximum fine of Rp. 25,000,000.00 (twenty-five million rupiah).

[DEFINITION FROM Article 126]
Article 126
Any person who intentionally:
a. using Republic of Indonesia Travel Documents to enter or leave Indonesian Territory, but knowing or reasonably suspecting that the Republic of Indonesia Travel Documents are fake or falsified, shall be punished with a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of IDR 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah);
b. using another person's Republic of Indonesia Travel Document or one that has been revoked or declared invalid to enter or leave the Territory of Indonesia or handing over to another person a Republic of Indonesia Travel Document that was given to him or another person with the intention of using it unlawfully shall be punished by a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of Rp. 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah);
c. providing invalid data or incorrect information to obtain a Republic of Indonesia Travel Document for himself or another person shall be punished with a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of Rp. 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah);
d. possessing or unlawfully using 2 (two) or more similar Republic of Indonesia Travel Documents and all of which are still valid shall be punished with a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of IDR 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah);
e. falsifying Republic of Indonesia Travel Documents or creating fake Republic of Indonesia Travel Documents with the intention of using them for oneself or another person shall be punished with a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of Rp. 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah).

[DEFINITION FROM Article 128]

b. any person who intentionally and unlawfully makes, owns, keeps, or trades stamps or other devices used to validate Republic of Indonesia Travel Documents or other Immigration Documents.

article_title: Article 138
full_content:
Article 138
(1) Applications for Travel Documents, Visas, Stay Permits, Re-Entry Permits and burden fees based on this Law are subject to immigration fees.
(2) Immigration costs as referred to in paragraph (1) are one of the Non-Tax State Revenues in the Immigration sector.
(3) Further provisions regarding immigration fees as intended in paragraph (1) are regulated by Government Regulation.

article_title: Article 139
full_content:
Article 139
(1) Immigration provisions for the movement of people entering or leaving the Indonesian Territory in border areas are regulated separately by a cross-border agreement between the Government of the Republic of Indonesia and the government of a neighboring country that shares the same border with due observance of the provisions of this Law.
(2) Immigration provisions for the movement of people entering or leaving Indonesian Territory using entry or exit signs using electronic means can be regulated separately through bilateral or multilateral agreements by taking into account the provisions of this Law.

article_title: Article 140
full_content:
Article 140
(1) To become an Immigration Officer, special Immigration education is provided.
(2) To take special immigration education, participants must have completed undergraduate education.
(3) The implementation of special Immigration education as intended in paragraph (1) is regulated by a Ministerial Regulation.

article_title: When this Law comes into force:
final_chunk:
[ORIGINAL Article 141]
Article 141
When this Law comes into force:
a. Visit Stay Permits, limited Stay Permits and Permanent Stay Permits issued based on Law Number 9 of 1992 concerning Immigration are declared to remain valid until their term ends;
b. husband or wife of a legal marriage with an Indonesian citizen whose marriage is more than 2 (two) years old and holds a limited Stay Permit based on Law Number 9 of 1992 concerning Immigration can immediately be granted a Permanent Stay Permit according to the provisions of this Law;
c. Republic of Indonesia Travel Documents that have been issued based on Law Number 9 of 1992 concerning Immigration are declared to remain valid until their term expires; and
d. Criminal cases in the Immigration sector that are currently being processed at the investigation stage are still being processed based on the Law on Criminal Procedure Law.
